title: ClassRoom 4-key second song chart
purpose: Second-song chart rebuilt on a stable 103 BPM quarter-beat grid, easier than Recruitment and clear for new players.
patternDesign: 103 BPM straight-beat revision with the first playable beat at 4.001s. All note starts are snapped to integer quarter beats; syncopated 0.5/0.75-beat placements were removed while preserving section density and pattern intent.

sections.name: prepare silence
sections.intent: no notes for the first four seconds so the player can get ready
sections.patternIntent: 4-second no-note preparation window; integer quarter-beat starts only

sections.name: ready intro
sections.intent: low-pressure warm-up locked to the first audible pulses
sections.patternIntent: second-song warm-up with wide spacing; integer quarter-beat starts only

sections.name: classroom groove
sections.intent: recognizable D F J K loops with fewer notes than before
sections.patternIntent: readable groove that follows the phrase accents; integer quarter-beat starts only

sections.name: after-school build
sections.intent: gentle build that rises without making the second song too hard
sections.patternIntent: soft ramp-up with breathing room; integer quarter-beat starts only

sections.name: clubroom highlight
sections.intent: fun highlight using modest chord rewards and a clean hold phrase
sections.patternIntent: small highlight, not a main-song difficulty spike; integer quarter-beat starts only

sections.name: quiet reset
sections.patternIntent: low-density reset; integer quarter-beat starts only

sections.name: return groove
sections.intent: return to the core motif before the final phrase
sections.patternIntent: simple return groove; integer quarter-beat starts only

sections.name: final bell
sections.intent: final phrase follows the last audible energy and then leaves silence before the song ends
sections.patternIntent: musical ending with no notes in the final three seconds; integer quarter-beat starts only

classroom_soft_call.description: Two-note call placed on stronger classroom vocal/melody pulses.

classroom_walk.description: Slow D F J K or K J F D walk with a missing beat for breath.

classroom_answer_chord.description: Small split-hand answer chord on an emphasized beat, followed by recovery.

classroom_hold_breath.description: Friendly hold phrase that leaves empty space around the sustain.

classroom_build_step.description: Gentle build with three to four notes, avoiding fast beginner-hostile runs.

classroom_highlight_soft.description: Main fun motif: D F to J K with a light chord payoff.

classroom_highlight_hold.description: Short highlight hold; K hold resolves into F+J to avoid awkward K overlap.

classroom_reset.description: Sparse recovery phrase used when the song drops in intensity.

classroom_final_cadence.description: Final cadence locks to the last audible classroom phrase and stops before the final three seconds.
